text: Higher foot arch is a risk factor for metatarsal stress fractures in athletes.
Abnormal foot inversion/eversion patterns are significant risk factors for metatarsal stress fractures.
Longer metatarsal length or larger metatarsal angles increase the risk of metatarsal stress fractures.
Long-distance running and recruit training are associated with stress fractures of the second or third metatarsals specifically.
Soccer is specifically associated with fifth metatarsal stress fractures, while running tends to affect the second and third metatarsals.
High exercise intensity, non-adaptive training progressions, and inadequate equipment are modifiable risk factors that heighten metatarsal stress fracture risk.
Personalized, multifactorial injury prevention strategies addressing anatomy, biomechanics, and training load are needed to reduce metatarsal stress fracture incidence.
